zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Stewart是Sir James Stewart和Catherine Kerr的女兒，於1562年出生在蘇格蘭皮布萊爾特拉華爾。她於1576年8月9日與William Burnet結婚。他們至少是14個兒子和8個女兒的父母。她去世於1625年12月21日在蘇格蘭皮布萊爾曼諾享年63歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭皮布萊爾曼諾。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Stewart was born in 1562, in Traquair, Peeblesshire, Scotland as the daughter of Sir James Stewart and Catherine Kerr. She married William Burnet on 9 August 1576. They were the parents of at least 14 sons and 8 daughters. She died on 21 December 1625, in Manor, Peeblesshire, Scotland, at the age of 63, and was buried in Manor, Peeblesshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Stewartは1562年にスコットランドペエブレシュアイアトラクアでSir James StewartとCatherine Kerrの娘として生まれた。1576年8月9日にWilliam Burnetと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも14人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼女は1625年12月21日にスコットランドペエブレシュアイアマノーで63歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドペエブレシュアイアマノーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Stewart(은)는 1562년 스컷랜드 피블레실르 트라퀄어에서 Sir James Stewart(와)과 Catherine Kerr의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1576년 8월 9일 William Burnet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1625년 12월 21일 스컷랜드 피블레실르 먼어에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 피블레실르 먼어에 묻혔다.
